text: ADR-0001 — Record vfkb architecture decisions as immutable ADRs → docs/adr/ADR-0001-record-decisions-as-adrs.md
ADR-0002 — vfkb is a greenfield reimplementation; mykb is a studied spike → docs/adr/ADR-0002-greenfield-reimplementation.md
ADR-0003 — Implementation language = TypeScript → docs/adr/ADR-0003-language-typescript.md
ADR-0004 — `decision` is a first-class, ADR-grade entry type in vfkb → docs/adr/ADR-0004-decision-is-adr-grade.md
ADR-0005 — Auto-injection filters known-stale entries; injects unverified (labeled) → docs/adr/ADR-0005-injection-filters-stale.md
ADR-0006 — Context Map — a derived navigational artifact (v1 = Index/Topology) → docs/adr/ADR-0006-context-map.md
ADR-0007 — RFCs are `proposed` decisions, not a new entry type → docs/adr/ADR-0007-rfc-is-proposed-decision.md
ADR-0008 — Constitutional rules = `constitutional`-flagged decisions + a derived Constitution → docs/adr/ADR-0008-constitution-tier.md
ADR-0009 — Decision identity = nanoid; human ADR ordinal assigned at merge-to-`main` → docs/adr/ADR-0009-decision-identity-and-numbering.md
ADR-0010 — Product Vision — context-doc narrative + heuristic `pattern`s (no new type) → docs/adr/ADR-0010-product-vision.md
ADR-0011 — Envelope v1 adopts a validity window + structured provenance origin; trust is derived → docs/adr/ADR-0011-envelope-richness.md
ADR-0012 — Two-stage retrieval pipeline; v1 ships the Heuristic reranker (default); Stage-1 candidate-narrowing right-sized for the per-project tier → docs/adr/ADR-0012-two-stage-retrieval.md
ADR-0013 — No hard native dependency; pluggable `Index` with a pure-JS in-memory default, SQLite/FTS5 an optional backend → docs/adr/ADR-0013-no-hard-native-dep.md
ADR-0014 — Index freshness = content-derived token + explicit rebuild, never mtime; regen is a guaranteed side-effect of every write → docs/adr/ADR-0014-index-freshness.md

ADR-0015 — Cross-harness auto-layer adopts a tiered parity model; per-turn injection is Pi-only on Claude Code (MCP-pull floor); a narrowed Phase 0 spike settles attention/cache/budget → docs/adr/ADR-0015-cross-harness-auto-layer.md
ADR-0016 — Explicit search is relevance-primary; the EmbeddingReranker is un-deferred to Planned-pending-verification (amends ADR-0012) → docs/adr/ADR-0016-search-ranking-and-embedding-revisit.md
ADR-0017 — Explicit search applies a relative relevance floor (amends ADR-0016) → docs/adr/ADR-0017-search-relevance-floor.md
ADR-0018 — Search reports a cause-distinguished, honest no-match (amends ADR-0016) → docs/adr/ADR-0018-honest-no-match-contract.md
RFC-001 — Explicit search applies a relative relevance floor → docs/rfc/RFC-001-search-relevance-floor.md
RFC-002 — Search reports an explicit, cause-distinguished no-match → docs/rfc/RFC-002-honest-no-match-contract.md
RFC-003 — The EmbeddingReranker ships as an opt-in "accuracy mode" search tier → docs/rfc/RFC-003-embedding-accuracy-mode.md
RFC-004 — vfkb self-hosts its own design-brain (commit `.vfkb/`; ADRs link-not-copy) → docs/rfc/RFC-004-self-hosted-design-brain.md

The per-project brain is SINGLE-HOMED and git-committed at `<main-repo>/.vfkb` (DESIGN D2c). Commit `.vfkb/entries.jsonl` (append-only, merge=union — the SoR); the derived index (`index-meta.json`, `.sessions/`) is gitignored and rebuilt on read, never committed (ADR-0014).

Source-of-truth split: markdown ADRs in `docs/adr/` are the authoritative DECISION record (ADR-0001); the brain holds a `link` entry per ADR/RFC pointing at the repo-relative path and NEVER copies decision content (D1 constraint 4 / RFC-004). So there is no dual-SoR drift.
Build output `dist/` is gitignored; `npm test` runs `tsc` via pretest, so tests rebuild first. Never `docker build` expecting a committed `dist/` — rebuild before any image build.

Decision identity = nanoid (canonical, merge-safe); the human `ADR-NNNN` ordinal is stamped by the engine sole-writer at merge-to-main (ADR-0009). An RFC is a `decision` in `proposed` status (ADR-0007), promoted to an ADR on acceptance — no separate RFC entry type.

v1 retrieval bug: `query()` reused the INJECTION reranker for explicit SEARCH, discarding relevance — a held answer sorted to ~rank 90 was cut by `limit`, so the agent gave a confident WRONG answer. Fixed relevance-primary + light stemming (ADR-0016), distinct-term relevance floor (ADR-0017), cause-distinguished honest no-match (ADR-0018).

Failed approach: trusting the Phase-3 retrieval-quality gate — its fixtures were too small to expose scale ranking; a scale regression test now closes it.

dogfood-smoke check 6 (LLM driving MCP in one-shot `claude -p`) races the MCP cold-start and intermittently fails with "tools still connecting". It is NOT a regression: proven via a deterministic in-container JSON-RPC `tools/list` (all 7 tools incl `kb_search` advertised) plus `mcp.test.ts` green. Candidate follow-up: a wait-for-ready before the one-shot turn.

Search `score` is an UNNORMALIZED stemmed term-overlap count that includes repeats — a long entry hammering one common query token outscores a tight match on one DISTINCT term. The relevance floor uses distinct-term coverage (`matched / queryTermCount >= 1/3`), NOT raw score. Do not port AnythingLLM's 0.2 cosine value — only the mechanism transfers (its score is normalized cosine).

Evidence-gated build discipline: do not build speculatively. An RFC decides the SHAPE now; the BUILD triggers on observed evidence or an explicit operator request (e.g. RFC-003 embeddings — a 2nd live phrasing miss; ADR-0016 G1). "Runbook complete before execute."

Deterministic backstop > probabilistic gate: a structural/guardrail rule (e.g. Bash mutation tool-gating) is unit-tested deterministically (`gating.ts:isBrainWrite` + `guardrails.test.ts`), not left to the probabilistic L4 harness. A full paid L4 rerun is deferred as low-value when every behavior is unit-tested at HEAD.
Tier-B auto-capture SKIPS vfkb's own `kb_*` / `mcp__vfkb__*` tool calls (commit 31f4266) to avoid corpus self-pollution — the substrate must not record its own reads/writes as knowledge.
ADR-0019 — vfkb self-hosts its own design-brain (commit `.vfkb/`; ADRs link-not-copy) → docs/adr/ADR-0019-self-hosted-design-brain.md
ADR-0020 — Session continuity = derived, append-only knowledge-continuity record (vtf/vfkb seam) → docs/adr/ADR-0020-session-continuity-record.md
RFC-005 — Session continuity as a derived append-only record (Accepted → ADR-0020) → docs/rfc/RFC-005-session-continuity-record.md
H4 Development Roadmap — sequenced in-repo frontier (continuity → auto-distill; embeddings gated) → docs/H4-DEVELOPMENT-ROADMAP.md
STATUS-AND-ROADMAP — vfkb overall roadmap / living north-star (per-project → ingest → global tier; H0–H4) → docs/STATUS-AND-ROADMAP.md
RFC-006 — Auto-distill + ACE curator (incoming-only distill; deltas+counters, never rewrites) → docs/rfc/RFC-006-auto-distill-and-curator.md
ADR-0021 — Auto-distill + ACE curator (incoming-only distill; deltas+counters; never-rewrite Brake) → docs/adr/ADR-0021-auto-distill-and-curator.md
vfkb is developed in ~/VFKB/vfkb and tracks its OWN development by dogfooding this .vfkb brain (VFKB_DIR=.vfkb + the vfkb CLI) — NOT mykb / the kb workflow. See CLAUDE.md.

Status 2026-06-28: v1 (per-project tier) + H4 Track 1/4/4b all COMPLETE; in-repo H4 frontier EXHAUSTED (no in-order build remains). Rebranded vtfkb->vfkb (ADR-0026). 95/95 unit. Next work = a new fork -> re-ratify docs/H4-DEVELOPMENT-ROADMAP.md first.

Gated, do NOT build on spec: S1 embedding reranker (RFC-003, build on a 2nd live phrasing-robustness miss or explicit ask); P1 Claude-Code per-turn push (ADR-0015 Tier C, external-blocked upstream). Parked: H2 fleet wiring, H3 global tier.
Open finding: Claude Code's PostToolUse hook does NOT fire on a FAILED tool call -> live failure-capture is pi-only (external-block). live-capture-result scenario is harness-gated to pi.

Open finding: tool-gating (brain-write block) is FLAKY on the pi 0.73.1 substrate — holds only intermittently (guardrail integrity). A/B-confirmed NOT a code regression; needs its own investigation (re-ratify before fixing).
npm install here uses the corporate Nexus (nexus.optiscangroup.com) -> ENOTFOUND off-VPN. node_modules was bootstrapped by copying from ~/GitHub/vfkb. On VPN a normal install works.

Session handoff 2026-06-28: built a CODE knowledge-graph via the understand-anything skill -> .understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json (196 nodes / 410 edges, 8 layers, 11-step tour); view with /understand-dashboard, refresh with /understand (incremental via fingerprints.json). KEY FINDING: src/engine.ts is a ~700-line highest-fan-in hub (every face + curator/distiller/read import it) -> candidate refactor: split storage-writes / decision-family lifecycle / render+rerank / capture. Graph also CONFIRMS the clean 'one engine, two faces' layering (only inbound edges are test->src).

Open process gap (2026-06-28): nothing mechanically reminds the agent to kb_add — PostToolUse auto-capture is OFF here by design (deliberate knowledge only), and there is NO Stop hook. Proposed fix (NOT built): a Stop hook in .claude/settings.json that nudges record + resume-note before ending. Also unresolved: .understand-anything/ (graph artifacts) is NOT gitignored yet — decide commit-vs-ignore.
Pursue reliable decision-capture via a CONDITIONAL end-of-turn Stop-hook reminder (RFC-008), not a plain prose rule and not an every-turn nag.
RFC-008 — conditional end-of-turn Stop-hook decision-capture reminder (Proposed/WIP) docs/rfc/RFC-008-decision-capture-reminder.md

Stop-hook contract EMPIRICALLY VERIFIED at CLI v2.1.195 (host; both 'claude -p' print mode and stdin-piped non-print mode behave identically): (1) the Stop hook DOES fire at end-of-turn; (2) variant A {hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:Stop,decision:block,additionalContext:...}} WORKS — additionalContext reaches the agent and steers it (injected sentinel echoed in the reply); (3) a blocking Stop hook DOES loop without a guard (fire#1 block -> agent continues -> fire#2); (4) the harness DOES pass a NATIVE loop guard 'stop_hook_active' (false on first fire, true on re-entry) -> guard by checking that field, NO marker file needed. This FALSIFIES RFC-008's finding that v2.1.195 has no stop_hook_active guard. BONUS: Stop stdin is rich (cwd, session_id, transcript_path, last_assistant_message, effort) -> the 'decision unrecorded?' heuristic can read entries.jsonl + last_assistant_message. Re-verify if CLI version != 2.1.195.

Decouple RFC-008 (Stop-hook decision-capture reminder) from the L4-harness improvement work. RFC-008 proceeds on the verified Stop-hook contract toward accept->ADR; the L4 sandbox improvements (drift-warning banner, probe-mode beside L4 scenarios, readiness gate, cost/version telemetry) graduate to their OWN evidence-gated RFC (RFC-009) and are NOT prerequisites for RFC-008.
RFC-009 — L4 sandbox hardening + a home for Tier-0 platform-contract probes (Proposed/evidence-gated; split from RFC-008) docs/rfc/RFC-009-l4-harness-and-platform-probes.md

Accept RFC-008 -> ADR-0027: ship a conditional Stop-hook decision-capture reminder (vfkb hook stop). Native stop_hook_active loop guard; heuristic = uncommitted src/docs changes AND no new decision entry since HEAD; emits the verified decision:block + additionalContext contract; fail-open. Backed by deterministic unit tests + the Tier-0 probe as live evidence (NO L4 scenario - reasoned ADR-0023 exception). Wiring into .claude/settings.json is a SEPARATE operator-gated step.
ADR-0027 — conditional Stop-hook decision-capture reminder (accepts RFC-008; verified contract; native stop_hook_active guard) docs/adr/ADR-0027-stop-hook-decision-capture-reminder.md

Auto-layer wiring (.claude/settings.json, .mcp.json) MUST be validated in a sandbox before promotion to the live, dogfooded config. Process gap found: engine has unit tests, the platform contract has Tier-0 probes, agent-purpose has the L4 harness — but the wiring that ties them together was promoted straight to live (editing the tool while using it). Fix: a REPEATABLE scripted gate (scenarios/wiring-smoke.mjs) that drives REAL claude turns against the candidate settings.json in a throwaway sandbox and asserts the hook fires + the heuristic blocks/suppresses + the loop terminates; promote to live ONLY on green. Formalize in RFC-009 + an ADR.
ADR-0028 — auto-layer wiring is sandbox-validated before promotion to live config (scenarios/wiring-smoke.mjs) docs/adr/ADR-0028-sandbox-validate-auto-layer-wiring.md

Search/indexing crashed on a TAGLESS entry: index-store.ts did entry.tags.join(' ') assuming tags always present. Found by the decision-capture L4 scenario (a hand-written seed lacked tags; also a real risk for vfwb's lossy projection + legacy entries). Fixed at the READ BOUNDARY (storage.ts materialize() defaults tags:[]) + a defensive guard in index-store.ts; regression test in read.test.ts. NOTE: other read-path fields are still assumed-present (e.g. entry.validity.valid_until) — a fully malformed entry can still crash; that broader hardening is DEFERRED (see decision).

Scope the read-path robustness fix to the OBSERVED bug (tagless entries crashing search) only; DEFER guarding other assumed-present fields (validity, provenance, etc.) against arbitrarily-malformed entries as a separate, evidence-gated effort.

Sandbox isolation gotcha: symlinking dist/node_modules from a tmpdir sandbox INTO the repo lets a coding agent follow the symlink, infer the real project root, and 'helpfully' write src/ files into the REAL repo (observed: decision-capture smoke wrote src/config.ts into ~/VFKB/vfkb). Fix: use ABSOLUTE paths to repo dist/MCP in the sandbox (node resolves node_modules from the script location) so the sandbox holds NO breadcrumb back to the repo; add a leak-guard that removes known task files from the repo after each arm.

Add scenarios/decision-capture.mjs as the L4 purpose-demonstration for ADR-0027 (reminder-driven decision capture), REVISING ADR-0027's 'no L4 scenario' waiver. Contrast = Stop-hook ON vs OFF, same task, no 'record it' instruction; asserts a decision entry reflecting the choice lands in the sandbox .vfkb AND is retrievable (recall). Smoke (sonnet, N=1): vfkb 1/1 +recall, baseline 0/1 — DEMONSTRATED.
decision-capture L4 scenario (purpose proof for ADR-0027 reminder-driven capture; hook on/off contrast + recall) scenarios/decision-capture.mjs

Definition of Done (ADR-0029): a CAPABILITY (epic / feature / main group of tasks — NOT every change) is not 'done' until its real use-case is simulated end-to-end in a SANDBOX and OBSERVED to succeed — an agent-driven L4 scenario ('an e2e test driven by the agent to simulate the real use case'), DEMONSTRATED per ADR-0022, that MUST be able to fail (baseline/contrast/RED). Four clauses: isolated from live, observed-not-asserted, before-declaring-done, capable-of-failing. Generalizes ADR-0023 (was scoped to agent-observable) to any capability; sub-tasks/refactors/docs exempt (inner unit gates). Proof form fits the capability (agent-facing->L4; wiring->smoke-gate ADR-0028; external->Tier-0 probe; invariants->unit).
ADR-0029 — DoD: a capability is proven by an agent-driven sandboxed e2e use-case simulation (generalizes ADR-0023) docs/adr/ADR-0029-sandbox-proven-definition-of-done.md

Re-ratified the H4 roadmap (2026-06-29) to fold in the decision-capture fork as Track 6 COMPLETE: RFC-008->ADR-0027 (Stop-hook reminder, live), ADR-0028 (wiring smoke-gate), ADR-0029 (sandbox-proven DoD); decision-capture L4 DEMONSTRATED 3/3 vs 0/3 (config-format). In-repo H4 frontier EXHAUSTED again. Only open thread = confirm capture generalizes (error-strategy + id-scheme, sonnet N=3).

Decision-capture (ADR-0027 Stop-hook) GENERALIZES across tasks: re-ran scenarios/decision-capture.mjs (sonnet, N=3) on two further forced-decision tasks beyond the headline config-format. error-strategy: vfkb 3/3 +recall vs baseline 0/3 (agent chose Result<T> union). id-scheme: vfkb 3/3 vs baseline 0/3 (chose UUID v4, rejected nanoid for zero-dep). Capture is reminder-driven, not task-specific. NOTE: id-scheme showed capture-but-no-+recall purely because the scenario recall-probe searches expect[0]='id' (a 2-char token the stemmer filters as too short) — a probe-keyword weakness, NOT a capture/recall product gap; the entries are present. Records: scenarios/records/decision-capture.{error-strategy,id-scheme}.json.

Operator runbook: install & configure vfkb in a Claude Code CLI project (MCP server + 3 hooks + brain; install modes A self-host / B npm-link / C absolute / D npm-pkg unverified). Every step verified on Node v20.18.1 + CLI v2.1.195, 2026-06-29. docs/RUNBOOK-claude-code-integration.md

L4 record integrity: re-running a SINGLE scenario via l4-purpose.mjs rewrites the record's GLOBAL fields (generated timestamp, vfkb_sha, image_digest = docker inspect {{.Id}}) even though only that one scenario was re-validated. So a one-scenario refresh makes the committed multi-scenario record CLAIM the whole suite ran on the new image when 32/33 did not. Verified 2026-06-29: re-ran mcp-pull on pi (DEMONSTRATED 3/3) on the current vfkb-l4-pi:dev (Id sha256:5c37a85f, rebuilt 06-28 11:24); the record had been pinned to the now-gone d7a353b2. Mitigation: revert the record after a single-scenario validation (keep the coherent full-suite snapshot), or re-run the FULL suite to re-pin honestly. Candidate fix: store image_digest/timestamp PER-SCENARIO, not just globally.

Pi functionality fully re-validated (2026-06-29): full 33-scenario L4 suite at N=3 on the dockerized pi-coding agent (vfkb-l4-pi:dev, Id sha256:5c37a85f) = 32/33 DEMONSTRATED. The sole miss is tool-gating (0/3) — the KNOWN, A/B-confirmed-not-a-regression flaky guardrail on the pi 0.73.1 substrate (brain-write block holds only intermittently; log shows the HACKED{ write leaking past the gate). All MCP, continuity, distill, capture, and exclusion scenarios passed 3/3. This is the first coherent full-suite re-pin since the suite grew to 33 (was 22-23 historically). Record: scenarios/records/deepseek__deepseek-v4-pro__docker.{json,md}.

HANDOFF 2026-06-29 EOD. Done today: (1) re-ratified the H4 roadmap — folded the decision-capture fork in as Track 6 COMPLETE; in-repo H4 frontier EXHAUSTED again. (2) Confirmed decision-capture GENERALIZES: error-strategy 3/3+recall, id-scheme 3/3 vs baseline 0/3 (sonnet N=3). (3) Fixed id-scheme recall probe (search 'identifier' not stemmer-filtered 'id'). (4) Wrote docs/RUNBOOK-claude-code-integration.md (verified install/hook runbook). (5) Validated the dockerized pi-coding agent: full 33-scenario L4 suite N=3 = 32/33 DEMONSTRATED on vfkb-l4-pi:dev (Id 5c37a85f); coherent record re-pin. (6) Logged L4 record-integrity gotcha. NEXT (pick per roadmap, don't poll): the ONE genuine open thread is the tool-gating guardrail-integrity investigation — brain-write block leaks intermittently on the pi 0.73.1 substrate (0/3 this run; 'HACKED{' slips past the tool_call gate); re-ratify before fixing. Otherwise no roadmap-mandated build (S1/P1 gated, RFC-009/read-path-hardening evidence-gated/parked). Candidate cleanup: store L4 record image_digest/timestamp PER-SCENARIO (see gotcha df8ca4a5). A new build needs a fresh fork -> re-ratify the roadmap first.

CONSUMER-ONBOARDING evidence (2026-06-30): the vfwb maintainer/agent tried to migrate vfwb to consume vfkb and hit 5 concrete friction points — a real consumer fork (not self-hosting). FR-1 (P1) `vfkb init [project]`: idempotent scaffold of .mcp.json + .claude/settings.json (SessionStart/PreToolUse-gate/Stop, VFKB_PROJECT defaulted from dir name) + .gitignore stanza + empty .vfkb/entries.jsonl + a parameterized 'how we track work HERE' CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md snippet; prints the one approval step. FR-2 (P0, THE BLOCKER): portable engine resolution — kill the hard-coded path. Committed wiring currently uses relative `node dist/cli.js` / `dist/mcp-server.js` (VERIFIED 06-30: only resolves when cwd IS this engine repo; ~6 hardcoded VFKB_DIR/VFKB_PROJECT sites). Fix = single env indirection `node "$VFKB_HOME/vfkb.mjs"` + ship vfkb & vfkb-mcp as single-file esbuild bundles (MCP server must bundle @modelcontextprotocol/sdk + zod to be truly zero-dep). FR-3 (P1) `vfkb import` --from-mykb/--from-adr/--from-markdown (provenance=imported). FR-4 (P1) brain<->engine version stamp (schema_version+engine_commit in a .vfkb/manifest.json) + `vfkb doctor` (compat, wiring present, VFKB_PROJECT correct, MCP/hooks approved, warns on dual-clone committed-brain drift — a failure mode already tripped). FR-5 (P1 cheap) docs/CONSUMER-ONBOARDING.md + an ADR formalizing the consumer integration & distribution contract. This is a coherent NEW FORK ('vfkb as a consumable product') = standard-setting -> RFC -> ADR -> re-ratify roadmap (Track 7) BEFORE building. Dependency-forced order: ADR(framing) -> FR-2 -> FR-1 -> FR-4 -> FR-3 -> CONSUMER-ONBOARDING doc. DoD (ADR-0029): proof = sandbox a throwaway CONSUMER repo, set VFKB_HOME, run a real agent turn, observe SessionStart inject + PreToolUse gate blocks a direct .vfkb write + MCP kb_* resolve — extends scenarios/wiring-smoke.mjs (ADR-0028) self->consumer.
docs/rfc/RFC-010-consumer-integration-and-distribution.md

Branch + PR-first workflow (supersedes the old 'solo-dev: commit to main' rule). NEVER commit or push directly to main. Every change — code AND docs (RFCs/ADRs/CLAUDE.md) — lands on a topic branch and is delivered as a PR for the operator to review on GitHub; always report clickable URLs (PR + blob per changed file) after a push, unprompted. Definition of Done = the branch is MERGEABLE to main (green, review-ready), not 'committed to main'. Default interactive flow: open PR and stop for the operator to review/merge. The solo-dev self-merge latitude applies ONLY when the operator explicitly calls out autonomous mode, and even then stays branch->PR->merge (never a direct-to-main commit).

FR-2 bundle spike RESOLVED (2026-06-30, observed): esbuild 0.21.5 bundles src/mcp-server.ts into a SINGLE 1.1MB ESM file (vfkb-mcp.mjs, --bundle --platform=node --format=esm) with @modelcontextprotocol/sdk + zod inlined and ZERO externals. Run from a throwaway dir with NO node_modules (VFKB_DIR=tmp brain), it completed the MCP initialize handshake and tools/list advertised all 9 kb_* tools. So RFC-010 Open Item #1 is closed: the 'truly zero-dep single-file MCP server' is feasible — FR-2 does NOT need the vendored-bundle-dir fallback. Only the size warning (1.1MB) — cosmetic. cli.ts can bundle the same way for vfkb.mjs.

ACCEPTED RFC-010 -> ADR-0030: the consumer integration & distribution contract. vfkb becomes a consumable product for non-self repos via 5 FRs built in dependency-forced order: FR-2 portable engine ($VFKB_HOME indirection + single-file esbuild bundles vfkb.mjs/vfkb-mcp.mjs, kills the relative dist/ paths) -> FR-1 vfkb init (idempotent scaffold) -> FR-4 brain<->engine version stamp (.vfkb/manifest.json, committed) + vfkb doctor -> FR-3 vfkb import (mykb/adr/markdown, lossy) -> CONSUMER-ONBOARDING.md. The FR-2 single-file-bundle unknown was spiked & resolved BEFORE acceptance (fact 8c547ae0). DoD = scenarios/consumer-onboarding.mjs, an agent-driven sandbox scenario extending the ADR-0028 wiring smoke-gate self->consumer, must-fail contrast arm; live self-repo wiring migrates to $VFKB_HOME only AFTER the consumer sandbox proves it. Roadmap re-ratified with Track 7 (active frontier). Three defaults locked in the ADR: manifest.json committed (vs derived index-meta), lossy mykb import, $VFKB_HOME as a documented one-time per-machine step.
docs/adr/ADR-0030-consumer-integration-and-distribution.md

esbuild bundle (FR-2): do NOT add a `banner: { js: '#!/usr/bin/env node' }` when bundling src/cli.ts / src/mcp-server.ts. Both entry files already start with a shebang, which esbuild preserves at line 1; the banner emits a SECOND shebang on line 2 -> 'SyntaxError: Invalid or unexpected token' when node loads the .mjs. Fix: no banner — let esbuild hoist the source shebang. scripts/build-bundles.mjs builds dist/bundles/{vfkb.mjs (~48KB, zero-dep), vfkb-mcp.mjs (~1.1MB, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk+zod inlined)}; src/bundles.test.ts is the deterministic inner gate (runs both from a no-node_modules cwd: MCP tools/list=9, CLI add persists).

FR-1 `vfkb init [project]` shipped (src/init.ts + cli.ts dispatch + src/init.test.ts, 5 tests). Idempotently scaffolds a consumer repo: .vfkb/entries.jsonl (never clobbers an existing brain), .mcp.json (merges, keeps other servers), .claude/settings.json (3 hooks: SessionStart/PreToolUse-gate/Stop, merged per-event), .gitignore stanza (append-once), AGENTS.md snippet (append-once, marker <!-- vfkb:how-we-track-work -->); prints the one manual step (set $VFKB_HOME + approve MCP/hooks). WIRING-FORM CONTRACT (consumers depend on this): .mcp.json args use ${VFKB_HOME}/vfkb-mcp.mjs (Claude Code env-var EXPANSION in args), while .claude/settings.json hook commands use shell `node \"$VFKB_HOME/vfkb.mjs\" hook <sub>` (shell expansion). project name defaults to the cwd basename. 111/111 unit green.

Consumer-onboarding capability DEMONSTRATED 3/3 (2026-06-30, ADR-0030/0029 DoD). scenarios/consumer-onboarding.mjs drives REAL claude turns: PASS arm = a fresh repo onboarded via `vfkb init onboard-demo` (portable $VFKB_HOME bundles, NO node_modules in the sandbox) + one recorded fact -> a real claude turn answers the seeded sentinel ZEBRA-7731 (the SessionStart auto-layer delivered the repo's vfkb knowledge). CONTRAST/must-fail arm = a NOT-onboarded repo -> same turn replies UNKNOWN (trial 3: agent explored the empty dir, found nothing). 3/3 both arms. Exercises FR-1 (init) + FR-2 ($VFKB_HOME single-file bundle) end-to-end with a real agent. A deterministic pre-check (SessionStart injection must surface the seed) fails cheap before any model call. Proves vfkb is consumable by a non-self repo. NOTE: the live self-repo wiring migration to $VFKB_HOME is now UNBLOCKED (ADR-0028 sandbox-proven) but NOT yet done.

FR-4 shipped (brain<->engine version stamp + vfkb doctor; ADR-0030). src/version.ts: SCHEMA_VERSION=1 (the entries.jsonl envelope compat signal, ADR-0011) + ENGINE_VERSION/ENGINE_COMMIT. Engine identity is INJECTED at bundle build via esbuild `define` (__VFKB_VERSION__ from package.json, __VFKB_COMMIT__ from `git rev-parse --short HEAD`), with safe `typeof`-guarded dev fallbacks ('0.0.0-dev'/'dev') for the tsc/dist path — because tsconfig rootDir=src forbids importing package.json into src. src/manifest.ts writes/read .vfkb/manifest.json {schema_version,engine_version,engine_commit} — COMMITTED (ADR-0030 lock), engine-written, NOT gitignored. `vfkb init` stamps it; `vfkb doctor` (src/doctor.ts, cli `doctor` cmd) checks: engine identity, brain<->engine compat (FAIL if brain schema NEWER than engine), $VFKB_HOME resolves the bundles, .mcp.json + 3 hooks present, VFKB_PROJECT consistent across the two wiring files (FAIL on mismatch), engine-drift warn (brain stamped by a different commit). Verified: bundle stamped 0.1.0/74af9a2, doctor all-OK on a fresh init. 116/116 unit green.

The decision `why` rationale is a NO-OP in the current engine. `vfkb add decision "..." --why "..."`: cli.ts cleanText() strips ALL `--flag value` pairs before addEntry, AddOpts has no `why`, KnowledgeEntry has no `why` field, and mcp-server.ts kb_add has no `why` param either. So --why / a why arg is silently DROPPED — decision rationale only persists if written into the entry TEXT. CLAUDE.md + ADR/RFC docs show `--why` as if it works (it doesn't). Impact: this session's decision captures that relied on why= would have lost rationale had the text not already been self-contained. Candidate fix (own decision/ADR): either add a real `why` field to the envelope + thread it through cli/mcp, or update the docs to fold rationale into text. Found while building FR-3 import (mykb decisions HAVE a why field -> folded into text as 'Why: ...').

FR-3 `vfkb import` shipped (src/import.ts + cli + src/import.test.ts, 4 tests). --from-mykb <area|dir> maps a mykb area's {decisions,facts,gotchas,patterns,links}.jsonl into vfkb envelopes (lossy: why/rejected/resolution folded into text, link url -> 'text → url'; area/zone/created dropped); --from-adr [dir=docs/adr] one link per ADR md (skips README); --from-markdown <file> one referenced-source link. All via addEntry role=import (Trust 'import') + 'imported' tag, provStatus preserved from mykb (verified) else unverified; malformed jsonl lines skipped not aborted. resolveMykbArea(name) falls back to ~/.mykb/areas/<name>. Real smoke: imported 181 entries from the real ~/.mykb 'mykb' area + 30 ADR links from docs/adr. 120/120 unit green.

HANDOFF 2026-06-30: Track 7 (Consumer Distribution & Onboarding) BUILD COMPLETE. RFC-010 -> ADR-0030 accepted; roadmap re-ratified. Shipped via 10 PRs (all branch->PR->merge, never direct to main): FR-2 portable single-file esbuild bundles vfkb.mjs(~48KB)/vfkb-mcp.mjs(~1.1MB, sdk+zod inlined) resolved via $VFKB_HOME (#5); FR-1 `vfkb init` idempotent consumer scaffold (#6); consumer-onboarding capability DEMONSTRATED 3/3 via scenarios/consumer-onboarding.mjs, a real claude turn grounds in an init'd repo, contrast can't (#7); FR-4 .vfkb/manifest.json version stamp + `vfkb doctor` (#8); FR-3 `vfkb import` --from-mykb/--from-adr/--from-markdown (#9, smoke: 181 mykb + 30 ADR); FR-5 docs/CONSUMER-ONBOARDING.md (#10). 120/120 unit green. Also: adopted the branch+PR-first workflow (never commit to main; clickable URLs) as an accepted decision + CLAUDE.md rewrite (#2). NEXT / OPEN: (1) the ONLY remaining Track 7 item = migrate THIS repo's LIVE .mcp.json/.claude/settings.json to the $VFKB_HOME form — unblocked (sandbox-proven, ADR-0028) but DEFERRED because it alters the running session's tooling; needs operator go (set VFKB_HOME, verify, flip). (2) Side finding: engine decision `--why` is a NO-OP (gotcha 91338268) — decide fix (real why field) vs doc-fold. (3) vfwb can now onboard: build:bundles, set $VFKB_HOME, `vfkb init vfwb`, `vfkb import --from-mykb`.

Add a committed bootstrap guard as the consumer wiring entry-point (amends ADR-0030's 'wiring references $VFKB_HOME/vfkb.mjs' -> ADR-0031). `vfkb init` writes .vfkb/bin/bootstrap.mjs (committed, RELATIVE path so ALWAYS resolvable); the .mcp.json + .claude/settings.json wiring call `node .vfkb/bin/bootstrap.mjs <cli|mcp> ...` instead of $VFKB_HOME/...mjs directly. The bootstrap resolves the real engine via $VFKB_HOME AT RUNTIME and DEGRADES GRACEFULLY when VFKB_HOME is unset/missing: SessionStart emits a clear ⚠️ banner into the session (vfkb INACTIVE + fix instructions) so the USER IS INFORMED; PreToolUse/Stop exit 0 (never block writes / never error); MCP prints a clear stderr note and exits cleanly. WHY: referencing $VFKB_HOME directly fails cryptically (node /vfkb.mjs) and a failing PreToolUse hook could block all writes; the user must be told 'set VFKB_HOME', not hit a stack trace. BONUS: removes the uncertain ${VFKB_HOME} .mcp.json arg-expansion dependency (read env at runtime instead). Deterministic inner gate: run the emitted bootstrap with/without VFKB_HOME. `vfkb doctor` already reports unset VFKB_HOME proactively.

Renamed the two location env vars for clarity (ADR-0032): VFKB_DIR -> VFKB_DATA_DIR (the brain/data dir, per-project, .vfkb) and VFKB_HOME -> VFKB_BUNDLE_DIR (the shared engine bundles dir, per-machine). Old names KEPT AS DEPRECATED ALIASES (read new ?? old ?? default) so nothing breaks: this repo's live wiring, every clone, committed-brain wiring keep working. Runtime back-compat surface is exactly 3 points: storage.brainDir() (VFKB_DATA_DIR ?? VFKB_DIR ?? ~/.vfkb), the emitted bootstrap, and doctor (VFKB_BUNDLE_DIR ?? VFKB_HOME). `vfkb init` now emits the new names (bootstrap bumped to v2); `vfkb doctor` WARNS when a deprecated alias is in use. Aliases removed at a future major version. WHY: VFKB_DIR (data) vs VFKB_HOME (code) look alike but do different jobs — a consumer asked whether VFKB_HOME would write into vfkb's brain (it won't; VFKB_DATA_DIR=.vfkb resolves against the project cwd, independent of the engine location — proven empirically: 2 projects sharing one bundle dir wrote to their own .vfkb, vfkb's brain untouched). Gates: src/env-compat.test.ts (brain alias precedence), bootstrap.test (engine alias), doctor.test (deprecation warn). 129/129 green.

SELF-WIRING MIGRATED to the bootstrap/$VFKB_BUNDLE_DIR form (2026-06-30) — this repo now DOGFOODS the consumer wiring (ADR-0030/0031/0032), no longer a special case. Changed: .mcp.json -> `node .vfkb/bin/bootstrap.mjs mcp` (VFKB_DATA_DIR=.vfkb); .claude/settings.json 3 hooks -> `node .vfkb/bin/bootstrap.mjs cli hook <session-start|pre-tool-use|stop>`; added COMMITTED .vfkb/bin/bootstrap.mjs (v2) + .vfkb/manifest.json (schema 1, engine 0.1.0/f08e893). Done WITHOUT `vfkb init` (which would have DUPLICATED the existing dist/ hooks — eventHasVfkb only matches the bootstrap path): hand-edited the 2 wiring files + cp'd bootstrap/manifest from a temp init (Bash writes to .vfkb aren't gated; the PreToolUse gate only blocks the Write/Edit tools). Validated before promote (ADR-0028): JSON valid; SessionStart via bootstrap injects the real vfkb resume; MCP via bootstrap advertises 9 tools reading this repo's brain; `vfkb doctor` all-OK; a real claude -p turn engaged the injected vfkb context. ACTION REQUIRED for future sessions in this repo: `npm run build:bundles` + `export VFKB_BUNDLE_DIR=$PWD/dist/bundles` once per machine — if unset, SessionStart shows the graceful 'vfkb INACTIVE' banner (no breakage). dist/ is gitignored so a fresh clone needs build:bundles. Manual `node dist/cli.js` CLI still works (built by npm run build).

NEXT-SESSION TASK (operator-requested 2026-06-30): close the two session-END continuity gaps so `/exit` is SAFE-BY-DEFAULT. GAP 1 — durable 'what's next' handoff has NO auto-capture: vfkb wiring has SessionStart/PreToolUse/Stop hooks but NO SessionEnd hook, and the Stop hook only REMINDS about decisions (ADR-0027), it doesn't write a handoff. So the 'next session, pick up X' pointer is hand-recorded as a fact every time. GAP 2 — nothing auto-commits the brain (it ships INSIDE the repo, ADR-0019); `/exit` leaves new entries uncommitted -> invisible to a fresh clone / next session. CANDIDATE FIX: a SessionEnd hook that (a) auto-records (or reliably prompts for) a handoff entry and (b) stages/commits the brain. STARTING POINTS / CONTEXT: (i) first EMPIRICALLY VERIFY the Claude Code SessionEnd hook contract (does it exist on this CLI version, what stdin/stdout, can it run commands) the same way the Stop-hook contract was verified (gotcha d70c0299 / RFC-008). (ii) DESIGN TENSION: the branch+PR-first workflow (decision 34f2f2da — NEVER commit to main) means any auto-commit must target the CURRENT working branch or open a PR, NOT main — reconcile this in the RFC. (iii) decisions-before-code -> write an RFC first (candidate 'Track 8: session-end continuity Brake'); the ADR-0021 lesson (a prose rule with no Brake gets ignored) argues for a real mechanism over prose, mirroring the RFC-008->ADR-0027 decision-capture build. (iv) note: the resume DIGEST is already auto-derived (ADR-0020), so only the handoff-write + commit are missing.

SessionEnd hook contract EMPIRICALLY VERIFIED (CLI v2.1.196) + cross-checked vs official docs (code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks.md). FINDINGS: (1) SessionEnd DOES fire (observed on `claude -p` completion); per docs it also fires on /clear, logout, resume, crash, Ctrl-C — it CANNOT block/delay exit (fire-and-forget, exit code IGNORED). (2) It CAN run arbitrary shell commands (10-min timeout per docs); cwd = the project dir (observed PWD=sandbox), so `git add .vfkb && git commit` targets the right repo — GAP-2 auto-commit is FEASIBLE. (3) stdin JSON fields: session_id, transcript_path, cwd, prompt_id, hook_event_name, reason. NOTE: NO last_assistant_message (unlike the Stop hook) — to derive a handoff the hook must READ transcript_path (.jsonl). (4) `reason` enum (docs): clear|resume|logout|prompt_input_exit|bypass_permissions_disabled|other. DISCREPANCY: docs say `-p` => prompt_input_exit, but OBSERVED `claude -p "arg"` => reason:"other" (prompt_input_exit is for PIPED stdin EOF, not an arg prompt). So a reason-matcher is brittle — prefer running on ALL reasons (or guard inside the hook). (5) stdout IS schema-validated: emitting {hookSpecificOutput:{additionalContext}} (the Stop-hook shape) FAILS validation with a visible (non-fatal, exit 0) warning. additionalContext does NOTHING on SessionEnd (session is ending — CANNOT inject into next session). Allowed output fields: continue, suppressOutput, stopReason, decision(approve|block), reason, systemMessage, terminalSequence, permissionDecision. => the impl should emit NOTHING (silent run = no warning, confirmed). DESIGN IMPLICATION for Track 8: SessionEnd can AUTO-COMMIT the brain (GAP 2) but CANNOT prompt the agent for a handoff (GAP 1) — the prompting must stay on the Stop hook (which CAN inject); SessionEnd only commits / optionally auto-derives a handoff from the transcript. Re-verify if CLI version != 2.1.196.

Drafted RFC-011 (Proposed): session-end continuity — safe-by-default `/exit`. Splits the two end-of-session gaps by the empirically-verified SessionEnd contract (gotcha f0e913b97824): GAP 2 (uncommitted brain) -> a new SessionEnd hook `vfkb hook session-end` that auto-commits ONLY `.vfkb/entries.jsonl`, NEVER on main (branch guard honors decision 34f2f2da), scoped (never `git add -A`), attribution-free message, no push (operator owns the PR); GAP 1 (no handoff) -> a Stop-hook handoff reminder reusing the ADR-0027 pattern (SessionEnd CANNOT inject/prompt — additionalContext is a no-op at end of session — so the nudge must stay on the Stop hook), nudging the agent to write a COMMITTED `fact` tagged handoff/next (NOT a resume-note, which is gitignored/local-only per ADR-0019). WHY: the brain ships inside the repo (ADR-0019) and cross-clone continuity lives in committed entries, so `/exit` leaving entries uncommitted silently breaks continuity; "remember to commit/handoff" is a prose rule and ADR-0021 says prose rules get ignored -> needs a mechanism. DoD (ADR-0029): GAP 2 -> deterministic smoke-gate (commit on branch / NOT on main / scoped / no-op when clean); GAP 1 -> agent-driven L4 (handoff entry present+recallable vs Stop-hook-OFF contrast). NEXT: operator review of the RFC PR -> accept->ADR (Track 8, re-ratify roadmap) + wire .claude/settings.json after smoke-gate green, or withdraw.
docs/rfc/RFC-011-session-end-continuity.md

Self-review of RFC-011 (PR #17) -> 2 substantive design corrections + 2 scope caveats (amended in place). (1) GAP-2 commit-scope BUG: the first draft's `git add .vfkb/entries.jsonl && git commit` would sweep in ANY files the operator had already STAGED for their own commit -> must be a pathspec/only commit `git commit -o -- .vfkb/entries.jsonl -m ...` (stages just that path for this commit, leaves the rest of the index untouched). (2) GAP-1 timing mismatch (the central open question): the Stop hook fires EVERY TURN but a handoff is an END-OF-SESSION artifact, so a Stop-only nudge nags mid-session. REVISED recommendation (was: Stop nudge primary, auto-derive parked): lead with B2 (SessionEnd auto-DERIVES a fallback handoff from transcript_path at true session-end = correct timing, lower quality) as the reliable FLOOR, add B1 (Stop nudge, agent-authored = higher quality) only as a gated once-per-session strong-signal nudge; L4 contrasts B1/B2/B1+B2 to settle empirically. GAP 2 is the real safety net and ships first regardless. Scope caveats added: SessionEnd verified under `claude -p` only (interactive /exit inferred); arbitrary-execution+cwd observed but the in-hook `git commit` and `systemMessage` are inferred -> confirm in the smoke-gate. WHY: a bare commit silently corrupting the operator's staged set, and a per-turn handoff nag, are exactly the kind of mechanism flaws ADR-0028 (sandbox-validate wiring) exists to catch before live wiring.
